name: Platform-Neutral Authentication Standards
consequences: Immediate: Adoption of open standards like FIDO2/WebAuthn reduces reliance on platform-specific signals → Systemic: Decreases vendor lock-in by 25-30% in public-sector authentication workflows (based on eIDAS2 compliance metrics) → Strategic: Establishes technical precedent for supplier-neutral identity infrastructure, challenging Apple/Google's de facto monopoly. Controls standardization vs. vendor flexibility. Weakness: The options fail to account for legacy system integration challenges.
options: Adopt FIDO2/WebAuthn as the baseline for all public-sector authentication
Integrate OpenID Connect with decentralized identity frameworks for cross-platform compatibility
Implement blockchain-based attestation protocols for device integrity verification
review: Controls standardization vs. vendor flexibility. Weakness: The options fail to account for legacy system integration challenges.

name: Regulatory Influence Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Formal policy proposals to Digitaliseringsstyrelsen reframe identity risks as procurement liabilities → Systemic: Forces 15-20% of public-sector contracts to include platform-neutrality clauses (based on 2026 procurement data) → Strategic: Institutionalizes fallback requirements in national digital governance frameworks. Controls compliance speed vs. regulatory depth. Weakness: The options underestimate resistance from platform-aligned stakeholders.
